seo.title: RabbitMQ Live Queue Monitoring — Real-Time Dashboard | Qarote
seo.description: Monitor RabbitMQ queue depth, message rates, and consumer counts in real time. Visual health states, 1-second refresh, and zero configuration — works immediately after connecting your broker.

hero.title: Your RabbitMQ queues, live. No configuration.
hero.subtitle: Queue depth, message rates, and consumer counts refresh every second — automatically, from the moment you connect. Three health states tell you at a glance what needs attention. No YAML, no dashboards to build, no Prometheus to operate.

capabilities.heading: A complete live picture of every queue

items.title: Queue depth
items.description: Current message count per queue, updated every second. Visual depth bar makes spikes immediately visible — you don't need to read a number to know something is wrong.

items.title: Message rates
items.description: Publish rate, deliver rate, and acknowledge rate per queue per second. Spot the moment a producer stops publishing or a consumer falls behind — without needing a separate metrics system.

items.title: Consumer counts
items.description: Live consumer count per queue. A queue with messages and zero consumers is a stuck pipeline. Qarote surfaces this immediately with a warning health state — no threshold to configure.

items.title: Health states
items.description: Three states: healthy (green), warning (yellow), critical (red). Thresholds are applied automatically based on queue depth, consumer count, and message age. The dashboard always shows you what needs attention first.

items.title: Node resource indicators
items.description: Memory usage, disk alarm status, and file descriptor pressure per broker node — visible alongside queue metrics. Node-level problems surface in the same view as queue-level problems.

items.title: Connection and channel counts
items.description: Total open connections and channels per broker. Growing connection counts are often the first signal of a connection leak — visible without a separate monitoring system.

steps.title: Connect your broker
steps.description: Paste your RabbitMQ connection string or management API URL. Qarote connects immediately — no agents, no plugins, no configuration files.

steps.title: All queues appear automatically
steps.description: Every queue on every vhost on your broker appears in the dashboard within seconds. New queues show up automatically as they're created — nothing to configure.

steps.title: Dashboard refreshes continuously
steps.description: Queue metrics update every second. Health states are recalculated on every refresh. You're always looking at the current state of your broker, not a stale snapshot.

whyManagementPlugin.heading: The management plugin is built for configuration, not monitoring
whyManagementPlugin.p1: The RabbitMQ management plugin's overview page shows aggregate metrics across your entire broker. It's useful for administration — not for watching individual queues during an incident. Finding a specific queue in a production broker with 200 queues means scrolling through a table sorted by name, with no visual health indicators and no way to sort by depth or rate.
whyManagementPlugin.p2: Qarote's queue list is designed for operational monitoring. Queues are sorted by health state — critical queues surface at the top automatically. The depth column has a visual bar. Rates and consumer counts are immediately visible in the list view without clicking into each queue.
whyManagementPlugin.p3: The management plugin also requires network access to port 15672 from your monitoring workstation. Qarote runs as a self-hosted service or cloud, connects to your broker over a single outbound connection, and gives your entire team a shared view — without opening your management port to every developer's laptop.

items.question: How often do metrics refresh?
items.answer: Queue metrics refresh every second. The dashboard polls the RabbitMQ management API continuously — there's no manual refresh needed. If your browser tab is backgrounded, polling pauses and resumes when you return.

items.question: Does live monitoring require any RabbitMQ plugins?
items.answer: No. Qarote uses the RabbitMQ HTTP management API, which is included with the standard management plugin (`rabbitmq_management`). If your broker has the management plugin enabled (the default in most deployments), live monitoring works immediately.

items.question: How many queues can I monitor?
items.answer: All plans support unlimited queues per connected server. Qarote fetches metrics for every queue on every vhost automatically — there's no queue limit and no configuration needed.

items.question: What's the difference between live monitoring and queue history?
items.answer: Live monitoring shows the current state of your queues — updated every second. Queue History (Developer+) stores snapshots every 5 minutes for 7 days, so you can review what happened during a past incident. Both use the same underlying metrics.

items.question: Does Qarote store live monitoring data?
items.answer: The real-time dashboard shows current state only — it doesn't store live monitoring data. Queue History is the feature that persists metric snapshots for retrospective analysis.
